title: azalea × rawsonii
intro: Azalea × rawsonii, also known as Rawson's azalea, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the heath family, derived from the cross between Rhododendron occidentale and Rhododendron dauricum. Traditionally, the flowers, leaves, and roots of Azalea × rawsonii have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Azalea × rawsonii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Azalea × rawsonii is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
